question.text: A call option seller receives Rs 10 premium on a zinc call with strike Rs 180. If the market price at expiry is Rs 220, the call seller's net loss is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Call seller loss = (Market - Strike) - Premium received = (220 - 180) - 10 = 40 - 10 = Rs 30 net loss. **The Trap:** Rs 40 is the gross obligation. Rs 10 is just the premium. Rs 20 ignores the premium offset. The seller's net loss is reduced by the premium received. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers short call payoff calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Call sellers face unlimited risk — a Rs 40 move against a Rs 10 premium illustrates why naked call writing is considered one of the riskiest options strategies.
